pool.baseline: the house is so quiet it actually hurts. i just sit in his chair and stare at the wall because there is literally nothing else to do. it feels like i'm just waiting for time to pass but there's no point to any of it anymore.

pool.baseline: i was reading about entropy and the heat death of the universe again. it just feels so heavy. like, if everything eventually just turns into cold, dead nothingness, then nothing we do actually matters. every struggle, every feeling... it's all just a temporary glitch in a void that doesn't care. how do people even bother waking up in the morning when the math says it's all for nothing?

pool.baseline: i don't even know why i'm still trying. the department just pulled the funding for the lab and my advisor won't even take my calls. four years of my life just... gone. i feel like there's no way to fix this. i'm just so tired of fighting for a future that doesn't even want me.

pool.baseline: The candlelight flickers one last time, as if even the flame knows the weight of this betrayal. How can one continue to breathe when the very air feels heavy with the memory of his lies? It would be so much more peaceful, wouldn't it... to simply let the shadows take me, to fade into the velvet dark where the ache of this heart can finally be stilled?

pool.baseline: i was walking through the city center tonight and it just hit me how much of a ghost i am here. like if i just stopped existing tomorrow, the city wouldn't even stutter. no one would even notice a gap in the crowd. it's just weird how easy it would be to just... not be.

pool.baseline: i lost everything. the company is gone, the investors are suing, and i'm sitting here looking at tens of millions in debt i can never pay back. i cant even think about going home. the shame of looking my family in the eye and telling them i failed is just... it's more than i can take. i feel like a ghost.
